reasoning:
1. Reference class: U.S. 3-year Treasury yield daily changes.
2. Base rate & Trend: A random walk implies a 50% chance of an increase over any given period. However, the yield experienced a sharp drop from 3.55% on Feb 11 to 3.43% on Feb 13. The FRED statistical tool estimates a ~71% probability of observing a higher value by the end of next week, driven by short-term mean reversion and the fact that 3.43% represents a local minimum relative to the recent trend.
3. Market News & Macro Outlook: Recent reporting (Reuters, Feb 12) indicates that while long-dated yields may steady or rise, short and medium-dated yields are expected to "edge down" as investors price in two expected Federal Reserve interest rate cuts in 2026. 
4. Synthesis: For the near-term resolution date (Feb 22), the likelihood of a bounce back from the recent sharp decline at 3.43% is strong, supporting a higher probability (68%). For the more distant resolution date (March 17), the overarching macro environment—which leans slightly downward on the short end of the curve—dilutes this mean reversion effect. Following the principle that uncertainty should increase with the forecast horizon, the probability for March 17 is drawn closer to 0.5 (58%).
